questions.text: The area drained by a single river system is called a:
questions.explanation: The area drained by a single river system is called a drainage basin.

questions.text: Any elevated area, such as a mountain or an upland, separating two drainage basins is known as:
questions.explanation: An upland separating two drainage basins is called a water divide.

questions.text: Which of the following is the largest river basin in the world?
questions.explanation: The Amazon basin is the largest drainage basin in the world.

questions.text: Most of the Himalayan rivers are ________.
questions.explanation: Perennial rivers have water throughout the year because they receive water from rain and melted snow.

questions.text: The Himalayan rivers perform intensive ________ activity in their upper courses.
questions.explanation: Due to steep slopes, they perform intensive erosional activity and carry huge loads of silt and sand.

questions.text: Lakes formed when a meandering river is cut off from the main channel are called:
questions.explanation: Meanders in the floodplains form cut-offs that eventually become ox-bow lakes.

questions.text: The Indus river rises in Tibet, near Lake ________.
questions.explanation: The Indus rises in Tibet, near Lake Mansarowar.

questions.text: Which of the following is NOT a tributary of the Indus in the Punjab region?
questions.explanation: The Satluj, Beas, Ravi, Chenab and Jhelum join the Indus in Pakistan. Yamuna is a tributary of the Ganga.

questions.text: According to the Indus Waters Treaty (1960), India can use only ________ of the total water carried by the Indus river system.
questions.explanation: India can use 20% of the Indus river system's water.

questions.text: The headwaters of the Ganga are called ________.
questions.explanation: The headwaters of the Ganga are called the 'Bhagirathi'.

questions.text: At which place do the Bhagirathi and Alaknanda join to form the Ganga?
questions.explanation: They join at Devaprayag in Uttarakhand.

questions.text: Which is the largest tributary of the Ganga that rises from the Yamunotri Glacier?
questions.explanation: The Yamuna rises from the Yamunotri Glacier and meets the Ganga at Allahabad.

questions.text: The Ganga and Brahmaputra rivers form the largest delta in the world, known as:
questions.explanation: The Sundarban Delta is the world's largest and fastest-growing delta.

questions.text: The Brahmaputra is known as ________ in Tibet.
questions.explanation: In Tibet, the Brahmaputra is called the Tsangpo.

questions.text: Why does the Brahmaputra carry less silt in Tibet despite its long course?
options: It is a cold and dry area
questions.explanation: In Tibet, the river carries a smaller volume of water and less silt as it is a cold and dry area.

questions.text: Which river is known as the 'Dakshin Ganga'?
questions.explanation: Because of its length and the area it covers, the Godavari is known as 'Dakshin Ganga'.

questions.text: The Narmada rises in the ________ hills in Madhya Pradesh.
questions.explanation: The Narmada rises in the Amarkantak hills.

questions.text: Which of the following rivers flows through a rift valley?
questions.explanation: The Narmada and Tapi flow through rift valleys formed due to faulting.

questions.text: The Tapi river rises in the ________.
questions.explanation: The Tapi rises in the Satpura ranges, in the Betul district of MP.

questions.text: The largest Peninsular river is:
questions.explanation: The Godavari is the largest Peninsular river with a length of about 1500 km.

questions.text: The river Kaveri rises in the ________ range of the Western Ghats.
questions.explanation: The Kaveri rises in the Brahmagri range.

questions.text: Which of these is a west-flowing river of the Peninsula?
questions.explanation: Narmada and Tapi are the only long rivers which flow west and make estuaries.

questions.text: Which lake is a result of tectonic activity in India?
questions.explanation: The Wular lake in Jammu and Kashmir is the largest freshwater lake in India, formed by tectonic activity.

questions.text: Sambhar Lake in Rajasthan is a ________ lake.
questions.explanation: Sambhar Lake is a salt water lake, and its water is used for producing salt.

questions.text: A lake used for generating hydel power is:
questions.explanation: Guru Gobind Sagar (Bhakra Nangal Project) is an artificial lake used for power generation.

questions.text: Rivers are considered the ________ of the economy.
questions.explanation: Using rivers for irrigation, navigation, and hydropower is of fundamental importance to an agrarian economy like India.

questions.text: What is the main cause of river pollution?
options: Dumping of untreated sewage and industrial effluents
questions.explanation: A heavy load of untreated sewage and industrial effluents are emptied into the rivers, affecting water quality.

questions.text: The 'National River Conservation Plan' (NRCP) was started with the launch of ________.
questions.explanation: River cleaning programme in India was started with the GAP in 1985.

questions.text: Which river flows through Ladakh, Gilgit and Pakistan?
questions.explanation: The Indus river flows through these regions before reaching the Arabian Sea.

questions.text: Most Peninsular rivers originate in the ________.
questions.explanation: The Western Ghats act as the main water divide for Peninsular India.

questions.text: The length of the Ganga river is over ________.
questions.explanation: The length of the Ganga is over 2500 km.

questions.text: Which river forms the 'Dhuadhar Falls' near Jabalpur?
questions.explanation: The Narmada river creates the Dhuadhar falls where it leaps over steep rocks.

questions.text: The Godavari's main tributaries include:
questions.explanation: Purna, Wardha, Pranhita, Manjra, Wainganga and Penganga are tributaries of the Godavari.

questions.text: The drainage pattern where a river and its tributaries resemble branches of a tree is:
questions.explanation: Dendritic pattern develops where the river channel follows the slope of the terrain.

questions.text: A radial drainage pattern develops when ________.
options: Rivers flow in different directions from a central peak
Rivers flow parallel to each other
questions.explanation: A radial pattern develops when streams flow in different directions from a central peak or dome like structure.

questions.text: The Sunderban Delta derived its name from the ________ tree.
questions.explanation: It is named after the Sundari tree which grows well in marshland.

questions.text: Which river rises in the highlands of Chhattisgarh and flows through Odisha to reach the Bay of Bengal?
questions.explanation: The Mahanadi rises in the highlands of Chhattisgarh.

questions.text: The Tungabhadra and Musi are tributaries of the river ________.
questions.explanation: The Tungabhadra, the Koyana, the Ghatprabha, the Musi and the Bhima are tributaries of the Krishna.

questions.text: Lakes are of great value to human beings because they ________.
options: Help to regulate the flow of a river
questions.explanation: Lakes help regulate river flow, prevent flooding during heavy rains, and maintain water flow during dry seasons.

questions.text: Which city is located on the water divide between the Indus and the Ganga river systems?
questions.explanation: Ambala is located on the water divide between the Indus and the Ganga.
